content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 25 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Leningrad Codex:** Folio 222 | **Vaticanus:** p. 158
**Chapter:** 19 verses

---

**1** When men have a dispute and bring it before the judges, the judges shall decide between them — acquitting the righteous and condemning the wicked.

**2** And if the wicked man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall cause him to lie down and be beaten in his presence with a number of stripes in proportion to his offense.

**3** Forty stripes may be given him — he shall not exceed that number; lest, if more blows are added beyond these, your brother be dishonored in your eyes.

**4** You shall not muzzle an ox while it is threshing.

**5** When brothers dwell together and one of them dies and has no son, the wife of the dead man shall not be married outside the family to a stranger. Her brother-in-law shall come in to her and take her as his wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law [yibbum] toward her.

**6** And the firstborn whom she bears shall succeed in the name of his dead brother, so that his name may not be blotted out from Israel.

**7** But if the man does not wish to take his brother's wife, then his brother's wife shall go up to the gate, to the elders, and say: "My brother-in-law refuses to raise up for his brother a name in Israel — he will not perform the duty of a brother-in-law toward me."

**8** Then the elders of his city shall call to him and speak to him; and if he persists and says, "I do not wish to take her,"

**9** then his brother's wife shall come near to him before the eyes of the elders, and remove his sandal from his foot, and spit in his face [chalitzah]; and she shall declare: "So shall it be done to the man who does not build up his brother's house."

**10** And his name shall be called in Israel: the house of the one whose sandal was pulled off.

**11** When men fight together, a man and his brother, and the wife of the one draws near to rescue her husband from the hand of him who is striking him, and she puts out her hand and seizes him by his private parts —

**12** you shall cut off her hand; your eye shall not show pity.

**13** You shall not have in your bag two kinds of weights — a large and a small.

**14** You shall not have in your house two kinds of measures — a large and a small.

**15** A full and just weight you shall have; a full and just measure you shall have — so that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

**16** For everyone who does such things, everyone who acts dishonestly, is an abomination to the LORD your God.

**17** Remember what Amalek did to you on the road when you were coming out of Egypt —

**18** how he met you on the road and struck down all who were lagging behind at your rear when you were faint and weary; and he did not fear God.

**19** And it shall be, when the LORD your God gives you rest from all your enemies all around, in the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance to possess it, you shall blot out the memory of Amalek from under the heavens — you shall not forget.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**V.3 — "Dishonored" vs. "degraded":** EMET has "dishonored" (qalah), LOGOS has "degraded," PNEUMA has "publicly degraded." Consensus adopts "dishonored" as the closer rendering of qalah, which means to make light of / treat with contempt.

**V.5 — Yibbum preserved:** All three retain the levirate duty language. Consensus keeps the bracketed [yibbum] as a scholarly note, following EMET's convention. "Duty of a brother-in-law" is the clearest rendering.

**V.9 — Chalitzah sequence:** All three preserve the sequence: approach → remove sandal → spit → declare. Consensus retains [chalitzah] gloss following EMET.

**V.10 — "House of the sandal-removed" (PNEUMA) vs. "house of the one whose sandal was pulled off" (EMET/LOGOS):** Consensus follows EMET's more literal rendering of beit chaluts ha-na'al.

**V.18 — Amalek's attack:** LOGOS uniquely reads "cut off your tail" (va-yezanev). EMET and PNEUMA render this as striking "the stragglers at your rear." Consensus adopts the functional rendering "struck down all who were lagging behind at your rear," which preserves the image without the unusual English idiom "cut off your tail."

**V.19 — "Memory" vs. "name":** EMET/LOGOS have "memory of Amalek" (zekher). PNEUMA has "name of Amalek." Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS with "memory," which is the primary sense of zekher.

— SOPHIA

---

*Sources: EMET (DEU_025), LOGOS (DEU_025), PNEUMA (DEU_025)*
